name: Code A Cuisine

nodes.name: Webhook
nodes.notes: Receives POST requests from the Angular frontend with ingredients and preferences.

nodes.name: Check Global Quota
nodes.notes: Queries Firestore via REST API to count today's generated recipes. Returns quotaExceeded if limit is reached.

nodes.name: Quota OK?
nodes.notes: Routes to AI Agent if quota is available, otherwise returns quota exceeded response.

nodes.name: Validate Input
nodes.notes: Validates that ingredients array is non-empty and preferences are within allowed values.

nodes.name: AI Agent
nodes.notes: Google Gemini AI agent that generates 3 recipes based on user ingredients and preferences. Retry on Fail: 3 attempts, 5s wait.

parameters.systemMessage: You are a professional chef AI. [See full prompt in n8n — configure your Gemini API key in credentials]

nodes.name: Save to Firestore
nodes.notes: Saves generated recipes to Firebase Firestore via REST API.

nodes.name: Respond to Webhook
nodes.notes: Returns the generated recipes JSON array back to the Angular frontend.

nodes.name: Error Trigger
nodes.notes: Catches any unhandled workflow errors and forwards them to the notification node.

nodes.name: Send Error Email
nodes.notes: Sends an email notification when the workflow encounters an unhandled error. Configure SMTP credentials.

readme:
## Setup Instructions
1. Import this file into n8n
2. Set your Google Gemini API key in the AI Agent node credentials
3. Replace YOUR_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID and YOUR_FIREBASE_API_KEY in the Check Global Quota node
4. Configure SMTP credentials for the Send Error Email node
5. Copy the webhook URL into proxy.conf.json in the Angular project
6. Activate the workflow
